**Arthurlie Football Club** is a Scottish [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") club from [Barrhead](/wiki/Barrhead "Barrhead"), [East Renfrewshire](/wiki/East_Renfrewshire "East Renfrewshire"). Based at [Dunterlie Park](/wiki/Dunterlie_Park "Dunterlie Park"), they play in the [West of Scotland League Premier Division](/wiki/West_of_Scotland_Football_League#Premier_Division "West of Scotland Football League"). The club played in the [Scottish Football League](/wiki/Scottish_Football_League "Scottish Football League") in two spells, 1901 to 1915 and 1923 to 1929.[[1]](#cite_note-C291-1) They have won the [Scottish Junior Cup](/wiki/Scottish_Junior_Cup "Scottish Junior Cup") twice, in 1937 and 1998.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arthurlie_F.C.&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Arthurlie was founded in 1874 and played as a senior side until 1929.[[1]](#cite_note-C291-1) The club reached the quarter-finals of the [Scottish Cup](/wiki/Scottish_Cup "Scottish Cup") in three consecutive seasons from [1880–81](/wiki/1880%E2%80%9381_Scottish_Cup "1880–81 Scottish Cup"), with their [1883](/wiki/1882%E2%80%9383_Scottish_Cup "1882–83 Scottish Cup") tie with Kilmarnock Athletic taking four games to settle.[[2]](#cite_note-2) A notable early result was a 4–2 defeat of [Celtic](/wiki/Celtic_F.C. "Celtic F.C.") in the first round of the [1896–97 Scottish Cup](/wiki/1896%E2%80%9397_Scottish_Cup "1896–97 Scottish Cup").[[3]](#cite_note-3) Celtic avenged this defeat with a 7–0 victory in Barrhead at the same stage the following season and the two clubs met again in Arthurlie's last Scottish Cup tie to date in 1929, with the Glasgow side winning 5–1 at [Celtic Park](/wiki/Celtic_Park "Celtic Park").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

With regard to league football, the club became a founder member of the [Scottish Football Federation](/wiki/Scottish_Football_Federation "Scottish Football Federation") in 1891, winning that competition in its first year before joining the [Scottish Football Alliance](/wiki/Scottish_Football_Alliance "Scottish Football Alliance") in 1893. Arthurlie obtained membership of the [Scottish Football League](/wiki/Scottish_Football_League "Scottish Football League") in 1901 and achieved modest results in the Second Division, with a highest finish of joint second in [1906–07](/wiki/1906%E2%80%9307_Scottish_Division_Two "1906–07 Scottish Division Two").[[1]](#cite_note-C291-1) That division ceased operations in 1915, during the [First World War](/wiki/First_World_War "First World War"), but Arthurlie did not immediately rejoin the league after the war ended.[[1]](#cite_note-C291-1) They instead waited until 1923 to apply for membership of the newly created Third Division.[[5]](#cite_note-C292-5) Arthurlie immediately [won](/wiki/1923-24_Scottish_Third_Division "1923-24 Scottish Third Division") the Third Division championship, and four reasonably successful seasons in the Second Division followed.[[5]](#cite_note-C292-5) Financial problems forced the club to resign its membership of the league with six games of the [1928–29 season](/wiki/1928%E2%80%9329_in_Scottish_football "1928–29 in Scottish football") to play.[[5]](#cite_note-C292-5) As the club had played all the promotion-chasing clubs, their results were allowed to stand.[[6]](#cite_note-C85-6)

A club of the same name joined the [Junior](/wiki/Scottish_Junior_Football_Association "Scottish Junior Football Association") grade during the early 1930s.[[5]](#cite_note-C292-5) They found success quickly with a [Scottish Junior Cup](/wiki/Scottish_Junior_Cup "Scottish Junior Cup") win in 1937 – defeating [Kirkintilloch Rob Roy](/wiki/Kirkintilloch_Rob_Roy_F.C. "Kirkintilloch Rob Roy F.C.") 5–1 in the final in front of a crowd of 23,000 at [Celtic Park](/wiki/Celtic_Park "Celtic Park").[[7]](#cite_note-7) Two unsuccessful final appearances followed, against [Fauldhouse United](/wiki/Fauldhouse_United_F.C. "Fauldhouse United F.C.") in 1946, then derby rivals [Pollok](/wiki/Pollok_F.C. "Pollok F.C.") in 1981, before they lifted the trophy again in 1998, taking revenge on Pollok with a 4–0 victory in the final at Fir Park with strikes from [Mark McLaughlin](/wiki/Mark_McLaughlin "Mark McLaughlin"), Johnny Millar, Steven Convery and Steven Nugent.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

In the Junior Super League era, the club were the first ever winners of the second tier [West of Scotland Super League First Division](/wiki/West_of_Scotland_Super_League_First_Division "West of Scotland Super League First Division") in 2003 and enjoyed an unbroken 15 seasons in the [West of Scotland Super League Premier Division](/wiki/West_of_Scotland_Super_League_Premier_Division "West of Scotland Super League Premier Division") until relegation to the newly configured [SJFA West Region Championship](/wiki/SJFA_West_Region_Championship "SJFA West Region Championship") in 2018.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-ogilvie-10)

The club's most recent trophy success was in the 2015 [West of Scotland Cup](/wiki/West_of_Scotland_Junior_Cup "West of Scotland Junior Cup") final, defeating [Kilwinning Rangers](/wiki/Kilwinning_Rangers_F.C. "Kilwinning Rangers F.C.") 4–2 at Newlandsfield Park, with goals from [Gary Smith](/wiki/Gary_Smith_(footballer,_born_1991) "Gary Smith (footballer, born 1991)") (3), and Ryan McGregor.[[11]](#cite_note-11) It was Arthurlie's sixth victory in the West of Scotland Cup, a feat equalled by Pollok in 2017 and only [Auchinleck Talbot](/wiki/Auchinleck_Talbot_F.C. "Auchinleck Talbot F.C.") (12) and [Irvine Meadow](/wiki/Irvine_Meadow_XI_F.C. "Irvine Meadow XI F.C.") (9), have won the tournament more often.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

The team are managed by former [Scotland Junior international](/wiki/Scottish_Junior_Football_Association#Scotland_Junior_international_team "Scottish Junior Football Association") defender, Duncan Sinclair, who joined the club from [Larkhall Thistle](/wiki/Larkhall_Thistle_F.C. "Larkhall Thistle F.C.") in June 2018.[[13]](#cite_note-13) Sinclair has also previously managed [Shotts Bon Accord](/wiki/Shotts_Bon_Accord_F.C. "Shotts Bon Accord F.C."), [Kilsyth Rangers](/wiki/Kilsyth_Rangers_F.C. "Kilsyth Rangers F.C.") and [Lanark United](/wiki/Lanark_United_F.C. "Lanark United F.C.").[[14]](#cite_note-14) He is Arthurlie's ninth manager since 2010.[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19)[[10]](#cite_note-ogilvie-10)

On 15 April 2020, Andy McFadyen become the club's new manager.[[20]](#cite_note-20) On 18 July 2022, Craig Palmer was appointed as new manager.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

## Ground

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arthurlie_F.C.&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Ground")]

In 1882 Arthurlie moved to the [first Dunterlie Park](/wiki/Dunterlie_Park_(1882%E2%80%931906) "Dunterlie Park (1882–1906)") from their Arthurlie Cross ground. They moved to the [second Dunterlie Park](/wiki/Dunterlie_Park_(1906%E2%80%931919) "Dunterlie Park (1906–1919)") in 1906, and the current [Dunterlie Park](/wiki/Dunterlie_Park "Dunterlie Park") in 1919.[[22]](#cite_note-22)
